TextENGNew: ``...There's no need to worry anymore.@k@vS14/10/21100079. Her condition has stabilized.``
Irie removed the stethoscope from his ears and turned back to us with a smile. The taut thread of tension inside me finally loosened, and I collapsed right where I stood.
``Rika-chan?!``
``Rika-chama?!``

Keiichi and Shion, who had been flanking me on either side, knelt down almost simultaneously to check on me. I tried to put on a brave smile for them, but I couldn't hide the cold sweat beading on my forehead.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``Is there another bed?!``
``There's one in the next examination room. Right this way!``
Responding to Shion's voice, Keiichi scooped me up. After leaving Irie's exam room, he laid me on the bed in the adjacent room, and Akasaka gently draped a blanket over me.
changeReason: New translation adds reference to shion, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: ``...Coach, how are Rika-chan's injuries?``
``We've taken X-rays.@k@vS14/10/21100082. ...Thankfully, there are no abnormalities in her bones.``
``But that Teppei is the absolute worst.@k@vS14/10/21100084. To use such merciless violence against a little girl like this...!!``
``...If I had run away, Satoko would be the one lying here instead.@k@vS14/05/210500845. So I'm glad it was me.``
I placed my hand over Irie's tightly clenched fist as I said that, and gave him my brightest smile. That seemed to catch him off guard, because he softened and gently placed his other hand over mine.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ``...I underestimated Satoko-chan's situation because she's so patient and never spoke up.@k@vS14/10/21100087. ...I will never make that mistake again.``
changeReason: New translation replaces detailed description of fear about Satoko's condition with a brief vow to never repeat the mistake, omitting emotional content.

TextENGNew: ...Indeed, it was terrifying.
Satoko suffered violence in the previous worlds too, but I could only ever watch from a distance, never truly comprehending the horror of her reality.
All I could do was watch helplessly as she was broken, unable to save her...
Compared to what she endured... this pain of mine was nothing.
``So... what about Teppei?@k@vS14/05/210500847. What happened to him?``
``Ooishi-san from Okinomiya Station is leading a considerable number of officers in pursuit.``
``...He said emphatically that they found a variety of illegal drugs when they searched the Houjou residence.@k@vS14/13/211300103. This should give Ooishi-san enough evidence to make an arrest.``
changeReason: New translation adds reference to ooishi, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: ``I wish they could've found the evidence and arrested him sooner, though.``
I felt the same way. ...But I'm sure Ooishi had been giving Teppei enough rope, on the suspicion that he might lead them to the bigger fish in the murder case.
changeReason: New translation removes curse/Oyashiro-sama reference present in original.

TextENGNew: Especially after Rina's murder. The police probably thought it better to overlook smaller offenses in pursuit of the bigger prize.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: And yet... why were they taking action now? It was a welcome turn of events, of course, but something about it nagged at me.
``Rika-chan.@k@vS14/13/211300104. ...Given the circumstances, are you really okay?``
Akasaka leaned close to my ear and whispered. I gave a faint nod in response.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ...My head still hurt a little, but I wasn't about to complain.
``Rika-chama, maybe you should rest...``
``Is Takano in today?``
``Huh? Oh, she already went home for the day.@k@vS14/10/21100089. Did you need something from her?``
``I'm fine.@k@vS14/05/210500850. More importantly, I want someone looking after Satoko.@k@vS14/05/210500851. ...Could I ask you to do that, Irie?``
changeReason: New translation adds reference to irie, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: ``...Understood.@k@vS14/10/21100091. Shion-san, shall we?``
If Takano isn't here, then nobody is watching over Satoko at the moment.
...With that in mind, Irie gently beckoned Shion and left the room. Shion, too, returned to the examination room with a reluctant look on her face, as though she couldn't bear to leave.
changeReason: Both describe Shion looking reluctant as she follows/returns; minor wording difference about direction.

TextENGNew: ...Now it was just Akasaka, Keiichi, and me in the room.
``...Who is he?``
``Keiichi is my partner.@k@vS14/05/210500853. My illicit paramour. Nipah~.``
``A-adulterous...?``
``Oh, I haven't introduced myself yet.@k@vS14/01/210101462. I'm Keiichi Maebara.``
Keiichi gave a polite bow as he said that.
After a brief glance at him, Akasaka turned back to me. I gave him a silent nod, and he turned to Keiichi with a look of quiet determination.
``I'm Akasaka, Metropolitan Police Department, Public Security Division.``
``...Oh, could you hold on a moment?``
``Uh, sure...?``
Akasaka stood up and pulled something resembling a black walkie-talkie out of his bag. He then swept the walls and floor of the exam room with it, checking for something.
changeReason: Different objects referenced (pocket vs bag).

TextENGNew: After checking all four corners, he set a chair in front of the door and came back.
``Uhh, what are you doing...?``
``Sorry about that.@k@vS14/13/211300109. I had to make sure this room isn't bugged.@k@vS14/13/211300110. In my line of work, I've always dealt with opponents who don't play by the rules.``
Akasaka shrugged and put the device away. So he carries a wiretap detector on him at all times. That alone told me he was a man accustomed to operating in dangerous territory.
changeReason: Translation differs significantly in the amount of detail conveyed.

TextENGNew: ``...Since Rika-chan considers you her partner, I'll trust you.@k@vS14/13/211300112. What I'm about to say doesn't leave this room.``
Akasaka placed a folding chair directly in front of Keiichi, close enough that their knees nearly touched. His unwavering gaze was enough to make an ordinary person flinch.
changeReason: New translation adds detail about Akasaka's intimidating gaze not present in the original translation.

TextENGNew: ...He must have endured some extreme situations over the past five years to develop this kind of presence. Keiichi, for his part, looked momentarily daunted by that intensity, but quickly gave a firm nod in return.
``So, first of all...@k@vS14/13/211300114. I should start with how Rika-chan and I are connected.@k@vS14/13/211300115. ...This is the story of a girl and a man who fell in love at first sight.``
changeReason: New translation adds the joke 'This is the story of a girl and a man who fell in love at first sight' not present in the original.

TextENGNew: ``...I'm going to tell your wife you said that.``
The words slipped out before I could stop them, and I immediately regretted it. Akasaka's wife was fine in the previous world, but I hadn't confirmed whether she was safe in this one...
However.
``Ahahaha, fair enough.@k@vS14/13/211300116. Even though you saved her life, you're going to get me in trouble? That's quite the sense of humor, Rika-chan.``
changeReason: New translation adds reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: Akasaka's cheerful laugh swept away my unease completely. ...Thank goodness. It seems she's safe in this world too.
Then, Akasaka explained to Keiichi how the two of us had met five years ago.
Akasaka had been dispatched to Hinamizawa by the Metropolitan Police's Public Security Division to investigate a kidnapping involving a minister's grandson--during the peak of the dam war.
He successfully rescued the minister's grandson from the kidnappers... and afterward, I confided in him about the deaths that I believed would befall the village.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to akasaka, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: It was not a short story, even in summary, but Keiichi listened all the way through with earnest attention.
``...So, Akasaka,@k@vS14/05/210500856. why did you come back to Hinamizawa?``
``I'm on vacation.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1024. After a few days of sightseeing in Hinamizawa, my wife, my daughter, and I are going on a trip south.``
changeReason: Old specifies 'wife and I' going to 'a private hot spring in Wakura'; new says 'wife, my daughter, and I are going on a trip south'--different destination and different family members.

TextENGNew: ``...But before that, Rika-chan,@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1026. there's just one thing I'd like you to tell me.``
``Mii. What... would that be?``
``...@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1028.You asked me for help back then.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1029. ...Is it still not too late?@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1030. Can I still be of help to you...?``
changeReason: Minor stylistic rephrasing; both old and new correctly convey Akasaka asking if there's still time and whether he can help.

TextENGNew: ``...There are many people who have no regard for human life.@k@vS14/27/100500179. ...Even if I tell you, there's nothing you can do to change the future.``
``But... if you want to erase the specter of death, sometimes, like with the reflection of the moon on the water, all it takes is a single ripple.``
I had no idea what she was trying to say.
I just knew... I had to absorb every word with my entire being. That's what I felt, deep down.
``And then the year after that, on this day in June of 1983...@k@vS14/27/VT4Bc_rika1080_2. ...or possibly the day before or the day after...``
``...I will be killed.``
``If all these deaths are preordained...@k@vS14/27/100500197. ...then the last one would also be preordained.``
This village is full of people with no regard for human life.
One could believe that all the deaths through 1982 were the work of someone in the village.
One could believe that all the deaths so far had been orchestrated at someone's discretion.
But that didn't explain what would happen in 1983.
There was no way the final death would serve anyone's purposes.
They had no regard for human life.
They would use any means to achieve their goals.
But their goals were at odds with the final death.
That's why the final death was unrelated to them.
But that final death would definitely,@k most likely,@k probably,@k without question,@k happen.@k Probably.
The final death would be a merciful one.
A cloth pressed over the mouth, consciousness fading, never to wake again...
Whose plan was that, exactly?
``...I want to live happily.@k@vS14/27/100500214. ...That's my only wish.``
``Surrounded by my precious friends, spending our days in joy.@k@vS14/27/100500216. ...Just that. Nothing more.``
``...I don't want to die.``
The girl, stoic as ever, let those final words slip at the very end.
...However.
Akasaka didn't understand the meaning of her words at the time. ...No, he chose not to understand.
To be perfectly honest, they sounded like the nonsensical ramblings of a child... and he was far more preoccupied with his wife's health.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to yukie, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: He was simply a narrow-minded man who couldn't see beyond his own problems.@k ...However.
Now, things are different. He has grown since then... and he has changed.
Because thanks to Rika's warning, he was able to protect what was most precious to him...
...That day, five years ago.

'Something bad will happen if you don't go back to Tokyo.' ...The night Akasaka heard Rika's ominous words, the first thing that came to his mind was his wife, hospitalized in preparation for childbirth.
changeReason: Both say Akasaka's first thought was his hospitalized wife; 'immediately thought of' vs 'first thing that came to mind' is same meaning.

TextENGNew: She might be in danger. ...He didn't truly believe it, but praying to God for an answer felt hollow and unsatisfying.
changeReason: Old says 'when he prayed to God for a safe delivery, he couldn't simply ignore it'; new says 'praying to God for an answer felt hollow and unsatisfying'--different emotional reactions.

TextENGNew: Unable to shake his worry, Akasaka called the hospital at some point during the investigation.
And he shuddered at what he heard from a nurse...
``...The rooftop?@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1032. What's she doing up there...?``
``Oh, please keep this between us, okay?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_nurA1002. She said the view from the rooftop helps her relax.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``She's made it a daily routine to climb those stairs every single day.``
...A cold, uncanny chill ran through Akasaka--more frightening than reassuring.
He couldn't laugh this off as a trivial matter. He was genuinely grateful to hear his wife was in good spirits.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: However... after that... the nurse mentioned the stairs.
Simply maintaining her balance while walking with her heavy body was exercise enough in itself. ...And she was going up the stairs to the rooftop?
changeReason: Old states generally that stair climbing should never be done at that stage of pregnancy; new reframes as a shocked question about going to the rooftop specifically.

TextENGNew: And to make it worse... doing it alone? Even if the odds were slim... if she were to fall...
A horrifying image surfaced in Akasaka's mind... of a nurse calling out to Yukie, who lies crumpled at the bottom of a stairwell...
changeReason: Old imagines 'a nurse going out to find Yukie'; new imagines 'Yukie crumpled at the bottom of a stairwell'--different imagery with more graphic detail in new.

TextENGNew: The tiles on hospital stairwells are especially slippery when wearing the sandals provided to patients.
changeReason: New translation omits the sentence about Akasaka having seen an old man nearly lose his footing during a visit.

TextENGNew: If she were to slip and fall... just like that elderly man had...
Any impact on a pregnant woman's body is dangerous. And not just for the child in the womb, but for the mother herself...
And Yukie was never one to sit still. Even with a fever, she would insist on being active without the slightest hesitation...
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: So, Akasaka...
``Huh...?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1032. What's wrong, dear?``
``Yukie... please.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1036. I know you want to move around, but I'm begging you not to do anything reckless.``
changeReason: Old says 'I want you to stay put until I get back'; new says 'I'm begging you not to do anything reckless'--different specific requests.

TextENGNew: ``Once-in-a-lifetime...? You're exaggerating.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1034. Did the nurse tell you something?``
``...@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1039.I'm truly glad you feel this way.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1040. But I want you to think of your body first, and the baby, before everything else.``
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ``Wh-what's gotten into you all of a sudden...?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1037. Did something happen, dear...?!``
``So, please.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1043. If something were to happen to you, I don't know how I'd go on living.``
``I want to build a warm family with you... together, forever.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1045. ...You might laugh at me for worrying too much.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1046. But I...``
Before he realized it.
Akasaka's words had begun to tremble, as if mixed with tears.
For some reason, he felt an overwhelming sadness... though he couldn't explain why.
Still... he needed to tell her. He simply had to.
He had just imagined the worst possible future. ...The sorrow he felt was so real... as though he had already lived through that loss.
``...All right, dear.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1040. I'm sorry for not taking better care of myself...@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1041. I promise I'll be more careful.``
``Thank you, Yukie...``
``Don't mention it. I'm really glad you feel this way.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_yukie1043. ...And you take care of yourself too, okay?``
And that concern... in a sense, led to the best possible outcome.
When Akasaka called a few days later, he learned there had been an accident where a loose tile caused someone to fall down a stairwell and suffer serious injuries.
And that was the very same stairwell Yukie had been using to reach the rooftop...
If he hadn't heeded the girl's advice and called Yukie...
He was awestruck. He couldn't believe it. ...It didn't take much imagination to see that as the worst-case scenario Rika had been warning about.
changeReason: New translation adds reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: It was all thanks to Rika Furude's miraculous prophecy...
``...And I hear the curse has caused mysterious deaths in the village every year since, just as you predicted.``
``Everything you told me was completely true.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1050. And the last thing you said...``
changeReason: Old explicitly states 'you were going to be killed this year'; new trails off at 'And the last thing you said...' omitting the specific revelation.

TextENGNew: ``Akasaka...``
``My wife and daughter are more precious to me than anything in this world.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1052. And my life is what it is today because of you.``
``That's why I will never allow the person who saved them to be unhappy!!@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1054. ...I owe you a debt I can never fully repay.``
changeReason: Old says 'let me use my strength to help you'; new says 'I owe you a debt I can never fully repay'--different concluding sentiments (action offer vs acknowledgment of debt).

TextENGNew: ``A-Akasaka...``
``......I feel like I forgot for such a long time.@k@vS14/13/211300151. ...But I finally remembered. Your words, and that promise I should have made long ago.``
changeReason: New adds specific detail 'Your words, and that promise I should have made long ago' about what was remembered, not present in original.

TextENGNew: ``...A-Akasakaaa...!!``
Tears came spilling out, unstoppable, falling to the ground.
Such encouraging words. The words I had always wanted to hear from Akasaka--the words I had long since given up on ever hearing.

``...You... You really did keep me waiting for so long...@k@vS14/05/210500861. And... in this final, final world...@k@vS14/05/210500857. it's so fortunate that you really came back...@k@vS14/05/210500862. Akasaka... Akasaka...!``
changeReason: Reverted: truncated: missing Akasaka...Akasaka...! at end

TextENGNew: ``I'm on your side, no matter what.@k@vS14/13/211300154. I'll believe any outrageous story you tell me.@k@vS14/13/211300155. So please, tell me everything.``
changeReason: Old concludes with 'I'll crush whatever foe is threatening your life!!'; new concludes with 'So please, tell me everything'--action promise vs request for information.

TextENGNew: ``Akasaka...... Akasaka... Uuuuuuu!``
Hearing that... Even though I had believed that a world without Hanyuu meant I was fated for the worst possible outcome... that despair vanished without a trace.
...I'm sure even Akasaka doesn't realize... just how much his words fill my heart with joy, gratitude, and courage...
But... I wanted to convey the depth and intensity of these feelings to him... and to Keiichi...
changeReason: Both say wanted to convey feelings to him and to Keiichi; new slightly truncates but core preserved.

TextENGNew: Right now... I have such powerful allies by my side.
There's no reason to be discouraged... No, I refuse to be discouraged!
I'll seize the future, for their sake too. ...I'm certain of it!!
``...The time to fight is almost here.``
I mustered my determination, then carefully told Keiichi and Akasaka everything I knew about Miyo Takano and this ``Tokyo`` organization.
changeReason: Reverted: hallucinated: JPN says about Takano and Tokyo org, NEW adds curse reference not in JPN

TextENGNew: And after finishing the explanation, Akasaka glanced back at the door behind him. ...Quite a lot of time had passed. I wouldn't be surprised if Irie and Shion walked in on this right now.
changeReason: Reverted: truncated: missing part about Irie and Shion possibly checking in

TextENGNew: ``So, Rika-chan.@k@vS14/13/211300158. Your suspicions are primarily directed at Miyo Takano?``
``It's more than just suspicion...@k@vS14/01/210101467. You're certain, right, Rika-chan?``
I nodded emphatically to confirm Keiichi's assertion. Akasaka seemed dubious at first, but he accepted it without question.
How reassuring. I had two allies who would accept such an outlandish story without the slightest hesitation.
``So... the issue is how to get the details out of Doctor Irie here at the clinic.@k@vS14/13/211300161. And how to find out more about this 'Tokyo' organization.``
changeReason: New translation removes reference to takano, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: ``Unless we can figure out what this ``Tokyo`` organization is really planning and take it down from inside, another person could come along and try to eliminate you, Rika-chan.``
changeReason: Reverted: wrong meaning: JPN says another person might come to eliminate Rika-chan, NEW says curse of Oyashiro-sama

TextENGNew: ``...Indeed. I don't know whose will is driving this curse, but I agree it's being done with malicious intent.@k@vS14/01/210101470. And it seems tightly controlled by someone.``
``Even if it is 'Tokyo's' doing, unless we can figure out why they want this year's curse to happen... we'll just be playing catch-up without a plan.``
Between Akasaka's experience and Keiichi's intuition, the two of them worked to piece together the mystery. ...But with so little information, there were limits to what could be deduced.
changeReason: Old ends with narrator being grateful to have allies; new ends with observation that limited information constrained their deductions--different concluding thought.

TextENGNew: ``At any rate, we need more information.@k@vS14/13/211300163. I'll continue investigating on my end. If either of you learn anything, please let me know immediately.``
changeReason: New translation removes reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: ``Got it.@k@vS14/01/210101471. Leave it to me.``

``Akasaka.@k@vS14/05/210500865. We'll also need the cooperation of Tomitake, who serves as a liaison to 'Tokyo.'@k@vS14/05/210500866. It's especially important because if things continue as they are, Takano will have him killed on the night of Watanagashi.``
changeReason: Minor nuance difference: 'Takano will personally kill him' vs 'Takano will have him killed'; both convey JPN 鷹野の手で殺されてしまう reasonably well.

TextENGNew: ``...Why would she do that?``
``I don't know.@k@vS14/05/210500868. ...But I believe something about Takano's hidden agenda is lurking behind the reason.``
``If that's the case, we need to protect him this time.@k@vS14/01/210101473. ...If we slip up like we did with Rina, it'll be too late.``
``That's true... but...``
...Be that as it may, I doubted Tomitake would take two kids seriously. And in the worst case, he might relay everything to Takano.
The ideal scenario would be for him to uncover the conspiracy on his own. ...Still, I couldn't think of a good way to guide him toward that.
``...Then, Rika-chan,@k@vS14/13/211300166. I've been thinking... how about this?``
``...Wh-what?!@k@vS14/05/210500871. A-Akasaka, are you really okay with that?!``
I gasped at Akasaka's astonishing suggestion. Right beside me, Keiichi's eyes went wide with shock.
``B-but if you do that, your wife who came all the way here with you will...``

``It's all right.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1056. I'll ask a colleague to look after my wife and daughter, explain the situation, and send them back to Tokyo.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1057. They can stay at her parents' house, and I'll activate the public security personal protection program — so there's nothing to worry about.``
``A-Akasaka... Even so, this will put you in danger...``
``Besides... it's just like I told you earlier, Rika-chan.@k@vS14/13/211300171. You saved my wife's life. So let me repay that debt by saving yours.``
``...I want you to trust me.``
``Akasaka...``
His eyes shone with unwavering determination. Bolstered by that resolve, I made up my mind as well.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Failure is not an option. Any misstep will put not just myself at risk, but Akasaka too.
But my decision is made. I won't hesitate any longer. I will never give up.
I'm going to seize my happiness, no matter what it takes... I'm certain of it.
Night had already fallen.
The bruises on my arms and legs still ached, but not enough to keep me off my feet. In this condition, I could manage Watanagashi without issue.
The village elders had rushed to the clinic as soon as they heard what happened to me.
They were so worried and agitated that they completely forgot they were in a hospital and had to be repeatedly shushed by Irie.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to keiichi/shion and adds reference to irie, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: Apparently, every last one of them planned to search the village for Teppei the next day. Their outrage at seeing Rika-chama beaten had united them in a way nothing else could.
changeReason: New translation adds reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: Seeing that, I decided to ask Kiichirou and the others to channel that energy into protecting Satoko instead.
...I could see the conflicted feelings on everyone's faces, but Keiichi and Shion stepped forward and addressed the elders directly.
``When people heard Rika-chan was seriously injured, so many came running to check on her.``
``I'm very happy about that, but I want everyone to think about this: if Rika-chan hadn't shielded Satoko, it would be Satoko lying in this bed right now.``
``I don't want anyone to think that would be any more acceptable just because it's Satoko instead of Rika-chama.``
changeReason: New translation adds reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: ``If everyone had been just a little more attentive and a little braver, Rika-chama would never have been hurt this badly.``
``As much as all of you cherish Rika-chama, did none of you ever consider that possibility?``
The elders had no response. Having witnessed yet another tragedy born from their averted eyes, their expressions softened with remorse and understanding.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...It would still be difficult to win their full support without Oryou's approval, but at this rate, Satoko would not be abandoned again.
And speaking of Satoko... I could only pray that she wouldn't remember 'that memory' when she woke up...
changeReason: New translation adds reference to satoko, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: For now... I had to set aside my worries about Satoko. I needed to deal with my own situation.
``Howdy, Rika-chan.``
``I'm glad you could make it. Nipah~.``
Tomitake had just arrived and was climbing the stone steps to the shrine.
From the camera in his hand and the bag on his shoulder, I could tell he had come straight from one of his photography sessions.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to irie, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: Just before leaving the clinic, I had told Irie I wanted to meet with Tomitake.
``I hear you wanted to talk to me... Is something wrong?``
``Tomitake.@k@vS14/05/210500877. Do you know anything about 'Tomitake Mark II'?``
I cut straight to the heart of the matter from the very first exchange.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: ``Huh? Wh-what do you mean?``
Sure enough, Tomitake's eyes went wide and he tilted his head, unable to grasp what I was getting at.
...At any rate, it seemed the nickname 'Tomitake Mark II' hadn't caught on. That was a little disappointing.
``Tomitake Mark II...?@k@vS14/08/210800005. What on earth are you talking about?``
``...A detective recently came to the Okinomiya Station.@k@vS14/05/210500879. ...Have you heard about him?``
``A detective in Okinomiya...?@k@vS14/08/210800007. Rika-chan, where did you hear about that?``
Tomitake was still smiling, but his eyes had sharpened and were fixed intently on me.
The Mountain Dogs had personnel embedded at Okinomiya Station.
So it was only natural that he would know about the new arrival, but he seemed surprised to learn that I was aware of it too.
changeReason: Translation differs significantly in the amount of detail conveyed.

TextENGNew: ``Akasaka is a friend of mine.@k@vS14/05/210500881. We became close when he visited five years ago, during the dam war.``
``Huh? O-oh... so that's what you mean?@k@vS14/08/210800009. Ahahahaha...@k@vS14/08/210800010. But five years ago is quite a long time.@k@vS14/08/210800011. You have a good memory.``
``Of course.@k@vS14/05/210500883. Because... he came here when the minister's grandson was kidnapped, right?``
Tomitake's expression changed completely. Of course, he likely already knew about the incident five years ago that involved the Metropolitan Police.
``...Which means... that detective...``

``...is from Public Security at the Metropolitan Police Department.@k@vS14/05/210500885. He came here looking for the minister's grandson five years ago, but what could he be looking for this time?@k@vS14/05/210500886. ...Hee hee hee...!``
changeReason: Reverted: hallucinated+truncated: JPN asks what he came to look for this time, NEW says came to investigate curse

TextENGNew: The smile had vanished entirely from Tomitake's face, replaced by the sharp, steely expression of an intelligence operative.
He was probably already thinking about staffing reallocations and future investigations with Public Security...
``Tomitake.@k@vS14/05/210500888. What's going on in 'Tokyo' right now?``
``What?@k@vS14/08/210800016. No, nothing out of the ordinary. Business as usual...``
``Akasaka told me a few things.@k@vS14/05/210500890. ...And he had some concerns about the flow of research funds at this clinic.``
changeReason: New translation removes reference to irie, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: ``Well, I might be able to look into that in the next few days...@k@vS14/08/210800018. But why are you bringing this up now, Rika-chan?``
changeReason: New translation adds reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: ``...It seems that the payments to Satoko and me are being funneled through Takano and Irie.@k@vS14/05/210500893. I thought the money was coming directly from 'Tokyo.'``
changeReason: New translation adds reference to satoko, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: ``What? That's absurd...?!``
Tomitake's expression darkened unpleasantly when he heard that.
One of Tomitake's responsibilities was overseeing how the funds were being used.
But he had probably been giving them a light touch, trusting in their cordial relationship. ...Whether the claim was accurate or not wasn't the point.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ``Understood.@k@vS14/08/210800021. This is a very serious matter if it's true, so I'll look into it right away.@k@vS14/08/210800022. ...Can I reach you at the usual number?``
``Oh. ...Tomitake, I do have one more request.``
Tomitake had cut the conversation short and was about to leave, so I hurried to call after him. When he tilted his head quizzically at the word 'request,' I pressed my palms together and spoke.
``Please.@k@vS14/05/210500896. I want you to keep this conversation a secret from Irie and Takano.``

``Irie and Takano have always treated us well.@k@vS14/05/210500898. I still can't believe it, and maybe it's all a misunderstanding.@k@vS14/05/210500899. ...I wouldn't want things to become awkward between all of us over a mistake.``
``...Understood.@k@vS14/08/210800024. I wouldn't want to strain our relationship over a financial misunderstanding, either.@k@vS14/08/210800025. ...I'll look into it as discreetly as possible.``
changeReason: New translation removes reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: ``Thank you.@k@vS14/05/210500901. I hope it goes well.``
I bowed deeply. When Tomitake saw that, his mood seemed to soften, and he spoke with a warm smile.
``Ahahahaha, leave it to me.@k@vS14/08/210800027. ...More importantly, Rika-chan, are you all right? I heard you were injured.``
changeReason: New translation removes reference to irie, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: ``I'm fine.@k@vS14/05/210500903. I'll see everything through to the very end, so go take lots of pictures.@k@vS14/05/210500904. Nipah~.``
``I'm looking forward to it.@k@vS14/08/210800030. I'll be right up front with my camera, so do your best. Good luck!``
Tomitake waved cheerfully as he descended the stone steps.
I sank down in front of the offering box. ...Keeping lies straight is far more exhausting than telling the truth.
But there was no avoiding it. Otherwise, Tomitake would simply continue trusting Takano, with no incentive to doubt her.
The first step was to make Tomitake suspicious of Takano. Then, if he managed to arrive at even part of the truth on his own, he'd become a powerful ally.
'Build suspicion from everyday doubts.' ...That was Akasaka's advice.
Tomitake had brought up the financial situation with Irie several times in the past.
'I do want to cooperate fully, but it would take too long to persuade our superiors. For the time being, if you could cover expenses through the clinic's budget...' ...Something to that effect.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to irie/tomitake, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: That had been born from genuine guilt on Irie's part. ...It was frighteningly easy to weaponize that guilt and plant suspicion in someone's mind.
changeReason: New translation adds reference to irie, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: ``Tomitake, Irie... I'm sorry for lying.``
Portraying a kind, selfless man like Irie as some greedy miser truly made my heart ache.
But I had no choice but to play hardball. If Tomitake didn't realize the danger he was in, he would die on the night of Watanagashi.
I looked up at the sky.
A beautiful full moon. Its dim light illuminated the shrine grounds and the dark sky, bathing everything in a faint, sacred glow.
Would I... be able to see a moon this beautiful in the future?
And, Hanyuu...
Honestly, where did that girl disappear to?
The moon wasn't going to answer that question.
I lingered there for a while, leaning against the offering box and pouring out the thoughts in my heart to the luminous moon above.
